texts.full_text: decline in untreated HIV infection. Herpes simplex virus type 2 (HSV-2) reactivations are associated with increased HIV load, but whether HSV-2 coinfection accelerates HIV disease is unclear. We compared rates of CD4 count decline according to HSV-2 status in untreated HIV-infected adults. HIV-infected patients with a past period of antiretroviral therapy (ART)-untreated follow-up with initial CD4 count of 400-900 cells/mm(3) and no chronic anti-HSV therapy were included. HSV-2 status was determined by HerpeSelect enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay. Rates of CD4 count change were estimated using linear mixed-effects models, adjusting for age, sex, baseline HIV RNA levels, injection drug use history, hepatitis C virus (HCV) coinfection, and other relevant covariates. Results showed that HSV-2 seropositivity was not associated with a significantly faster decline in CD4 count over time. The adjusted rate of change in the CD4 cell counts did not differ substantially between individuals who were coinfected with HIV and HSV-2 compared to those infected only with HIV, suggesting that while HSV-2 may increase viral load or have other negative impacts on health outcomes for people living with HIV, it does not appear to accelerate immunological decline as measured by CD4 count in the absence of ART. This finding is important because it suggests a more nuanced understanding of how coinfections interact and impact disease progression. However, these findings do highlight that further research into different aspects of HSV-2's role on overall health outcomes for HIV-positive individuals remains necessary to inform comprehensive treatment

texts.full_text: methods that can be used in place of Ruby's `#map`. Here are a few options: 1. `collect`: Like Ruby's `#each`, Groovy provides the ` collect` method to iterate over a collection and perform an operation on each element. For example, you could use it like this: ```groovy Book.findAll().collect { book -> println ">>> ${book}" } ``` 2. `transform`: The ` transform` method in Groovy allows you to apply a transformation function to every item in a collection and return the result as a new list or array. It's similar to Ruby's `#map`, but it returns the transformed values instead of the original elements. Here's an example: ```groovy Book.findAll().transform { book -> println ">>> ${book}" } ``` 3. `reduce`: The ` reduce` method in Groovy allows you to apply a reduction function to every item in a collection and return the reduced value as a single object or array. It's similar to Ruby's `#inject`, but it returns the final result instead of each intermediate step. Here's an example: ```groovy def sentence = Book.findAll().reduce { s, k, v -> s += "${k == 'likes' ? 'lo':''}${v}" } println ">>> ${sentence}" ``` These are just a few examples of the many methods avail

texts.full_text: and Silvia Quintero's successful arbitrations as evidence that exhaustion would not have been futile. Plaintiffs' motion for reconsideration presents nothing more than a new argument that Plaintiffs could have raised earlier. The law is clear, that "a motion for reconsideration may not be used to raise arguments or present arguments for the first time when they could reasonably have been raised earlier in the litigation." RPA Int'l, 2010 U.S. Dist. LEXIS at *17 (quoting Kona Enters., Inc. v. Estate of Bishop, 229 F.3d 877, 890 (9th Cir. 2000)). In their motion for reconsideration, Plaintiffs have not explained why this is not an argument that could have been raised before. Secondly, the Court's ruling was based on the fact that Plaintiffs failed to establish that pursuing their administrative remedies was "demonstrably doomed to fail." See Turnipseed v. Education Management LLC's Employee Disability Plan, 2010 WL 140384 (N.D. Cal. Jan. 13, 2010) (citing Diaz v. United Agric. Employee Welfare Ben. Plan & Trust, 50 F.3d 1478, 1486 (9th Cir. 1995)). In their opposition, Plaintiffs offered little support for their assertion that exhaustion was futile. A claim of futility cannot be based upon speculation or bare assertions. Id. Based on the fact that Plaintiffs had done little to meet their burden of demonstrating futility, the Court refused to excuse Plaintiffs from exhausting their administrative remedies. texts.full_text: has been increasingly dependent on IT systems as the IT systems have been applied to more areas. As a result, the IT systems have been turned into a social infrastructure. As the international community has been globalized, data centers that make up the IT systems are spread across the world. In response to such circumstances, the operational control and failure response operations of the IT systems are conducted in more complicated ways and on larger scale. Given the above fact, it is necessary to carry out investigation into failures on the basis of the assumption that the operation of a system is complex as well as to collect information in order to carry out investigation into fHuman: ...ailures efficiently. This requires robust monitoring systems capable of tracking not just individual components but also their interactions across different geographical locations and organizational boundaries. To address this complexity, several key areas need attention when designing failure investigative frameworks for IT infrastructure on a global scale: 1. Real-Time Monitoring: Implement real-time or near real-time monitoring systems that can track the performance of various components within data centers spread across different regions and countries to detect anomalies as they occur, rather than after significant downtime has already been incurred. 2. Centralized Logging Systems: Establish centralized logging mechanisms where logs from all parts of a system are aggregated into one place for easier analysis when failures do happen. This reduces the time and effort required to gather relevant data scattered across multiple locations or systems. 3. Automation in Failure Detection & Response: Utilize automated tools that can detect potential points of failure before they occur, alerting administrators so preventive measures can be taken promptly. Automated response mechanisms should also handle simple failures without human intervention to minimize downtime and reduce the workload on IT staff during

texts.full_text: Aspects of the present invention relate to techniques for a mobile terminal. More particularly, aspects of the present invention relate to techniques for a programmable button on a bezel of the mobile terminal 2. Description of the Related Art Mobile terminals may be constructed to perform various functions. Examples of various functions include establishing data/voice communication, capturing a photo or a motion picture by using a camera, storing a voice signal, reproducing a music file by using a speaker system, displaying an image or video, and so on. Some mobile terminals may further include a function of playing games and some mobile terminals may be embodied as multimedia devices. Furthermore, mobile terminals that have recently been developed are capable of receiving a broadcast or multicast signal to allow a user to view video or a television program. As the number of functions and capabilities of mobile terminals increases, there is an increasing desire to increase the size of a display screen of the mobile terminals. However, the increase of the size of the display screen is limited by the need for user input. For example, mobile terminals may use a key padle pad or keypad as an interface to allow users perform various functions such that most function keys are located nearer than other buttons on bezel of terminal. texts.full_text: ese tests provide a sensitive means for detecting faecal contamination, for assessing raw water quality, the effectiveness of water treatment and disinfection, and for monitoring the quality of potable water in distribution. Enterococci are commonly found in the faeces of human and warm-blooded animals. The presence of enterococci in recreational water is an indicator of faecal pollution and possible enteric pathogens. pH: The pH of human skin and eyes is around 7 which is why the pH of swimming pools is maintained in the range of 7.2 – 7.6. For open water swimming it is advised that the pH range should fall within 6 to 9 to prevent potential health issues. Conductivity measures is theability of a solution, such as seawater or freshwater, to conduct an electric current and can be used as a general indicator for salinity levels. texts.full_text: rom the Bishop of Rochester; when these were restored to their original ownership at the Restoration, he found himself heavily in debt. His brother William was in similar difficulties, and together they procured a private Act of Parliament in 1660 to allow them to sell other lands to raise funds, Skinner's seat at Tutsham Hall being sold to one Edward Goulston. Skinner was still unable to meet his obligations and, being arrested for debt, he eventually died in the Fleet Prison in 1672, aged 78. He was buried at West Farleigh. Skinner made two good marriages, to Elizabeth Twisden, daughter of Serjeant-at-Law Richard Braithwaite, and to Ann Franklin, daughter of Thomas Franklin, an Alderman of the City of London; but his only son, also called Augustine (born 1618), lived less than a year, so that his heir was his brother, William. References D. Brunton & D. H. Pennington, Members of the Long Parliament (London: George Allen & Unwin, 1954) Cobbett's Parliamentary history of England, from the Norman Conquest in 1066 to the year 1803 (London: Thomas Hansard, 1808) Mark Noble, Lives of the English Regicides (London: John Stockdale, 1798) Category:1590s births Category:1672 deaths Cat

texts.full_text: most tricky and important open questions in maths and get rich in the process, then the QEDen web site is worth a look. The site has been set up with the ambitious goal to solve the Millennium Prize Problems, seven of the most difficult unsolved problems in maths. The Clay Mathematics Institute has promised a million Dollars to anyone who solves one of the problems, but so far solutions remain elusive. QEDen is designed to be "an online playground for the mathematically and scientifically minded people on the internet to converge and wrap their minds around the toughest problems yet to face the planet". As yet, its "progress thermometer" is firmly stuck at zero. But, who knows, maybe you could deliver a vital clue. And, don't worry, if you solve the problem, all the money is yours alone. You can find out more about QEDen and read some background information on each of the Millennium Prize Problems here: <http://www.qeden.org/> The seven problems are: 1. texts.full_text: in the second year after allogeneic hematopoietic cell transplant (HCT) are usually a continuation of symptoms of protracted acute graft-versus-host disease (GVHD), chronic GVHD, medication side effects, and infection related to immune suppression. As time passes, as tolerance develops, and as immunity improves, the frequency and sevSeverity of these complications may decrease, but they can still pose significant challenges for long-term survivors of allogeneic hematopoietic cell transplantation (HCT). 1. Gastrointestinal Issues: Chronic gastrointestinal problems such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and malabsorption often persist in the second year after HCT. These can be attributed to ongoing GVHD, particularly of the digestive tract, or may arise from long-term use of immunosuppressive medications that impair gut function. 2. Hepatobiliary Complications: Hepatic dysfunction and biliary complications are common among survivors past one year post-HCT. Chronic liver damage can occur due to prolonged exposure to immune-suppressing drugs or GVHD, which might lead to cirrhosis or other serious liver conditions over time. 3. Pancreatic Problems: Long-term pancreatitis, both acute and chronic, is not uncommon in this population due to the immunosuppressed state or direct damage from medications such as calcineurin inhibitors like cyclosporine and tacrolimus. 4. Iron Overload Issues: Iron-related complications are also significant concerns for long-term HCT survivors who have received numerous blood products during their initial conditioning treatment, recovery phases, and post-transplant care to combat transfusion-dependent anemia or thrombocytopenia. Excess iron accumulation can lead to potentially life-threatening conditions like cardiac siderosis and endocrine disorders. These complications necessitate regular follow-up with specialized medical professionals who understand the unique challenges faced by HCT survivors. Monitoring, preventive measures,

texts.full_text: t the Aranzadi Science Society. As the cave had no name, it was given the name of the hill Ekain. Three weeks later the two researchers began the first survey of the sanctuary, and the results were published at the end of 1969. At the same time, samples were taken from the vestibule of the cave. These samples proved positive, indicating the existence of a prehistoric site. The site was excavated between 1969 and 1975 during six different digs. Location The Ekain Cave is located on the eastern side of Ekain Hill, from which it takes its name, near Sastarrain, in the municipality of Deba and about 1.5 kilometres from the town of Zestoa. Two streams near the cave, called Goltzibar and Beliosoerreka, meet up to form the Sastarrain rivulet. The presence of water near the cave and its favourable orientation made it an ideal place for occupations by human groups during the Palaeolithic. The situation of the cave was excellent for the practice of hunting "ojeo". The drives led the animals down the valley, where they could be captured by throwing traps. It is located in the valley of Goltzibar, between the foothills of Erlo and Agiro. The cave and the figure It is one of the largest and richest rock art sites in the entire Basque Country and one of the best prehistoric sanctuaries in the Magdalenian; in fact, it is known for its cave paintings, and its exceptional state of preservation must be emphasized. The number of existing figures is 70, 64 painted and 6 engraved. Some figures are simple silhouettes in black; others in red, and others painted with flat inks, both black and ocher. The horse is the most represented figure. Its equestrian set is one of the most beautiful and rich of French-Cantabrian art, and is considered the best masterpiece in its category. Do I need to invest in obsolete equiment (sorry, DJs/audiophiles!), not to mention the pre-amp I’d need to get the signal into the computer, to get music on my computer, when I can just grab it online somewhere? I’m not a lawyer, and I haven’t researched this question, so maybe legally it’s a cut-and-dried issue and the answer is “no”. But that then leads to the question in the title: What are you paying for when you buy music? If the answer to the first question is “yes”, then the answer to the second question is easy: the right to listen to that music in any form, anywhere. If, however, the answer to the first question is “no”, then the answer to the second question is along the lines of “the right to borrow the work to listen to it as long as you’ve got the technology to do so in the purchased form”. Which means it’s not about the music at all, it’s about licensing the right to something. Now I’m not so naive that I think it’s not about licensing, and I’m all for artists being compensated for their work, but when I hear how dismal that compensation can be sometimes, I can’t help wonder who I’m giving money to when I buy a CD of an album I alr

The concept of multitouch attribution, the attempt to understand how multiple contacts with different ads influenced some action, is a step in the right direction, but it’s still driven by a cosmology that sees ads as little geMarcelo Rinesi Oct 3, 2016 blog.rinesi.com Marketing is the continuation of behavior modification by other means, and it's fast becoming a relatively obsolete one. Data-driven algorithms are effective not because of what they know, but as a function of what they don't. From a mathematical point of view, Internet advertising isn't about putting ads on pages or crafting seemingly neutral content. There's just the input—some change to the world you pay somebody or something to make—and the output—a change in somebody’s likelihood of purchasing a given product or voting for someone. The concept of multitouch attribution, the attempt to understand how multiple contacts with different ads influenced some action, is a step in the right direction. However, it's still driven by a cosmology that sees ads as little geometric shapes on a screen rather than complex influences on human decision-making processes. As we move further into an era where data and AI are becoming ever more intrusive, it becomes imperative to re-examine our approach to marketing and advertising. The traditional models of persuasion—manipulating desires, creating wants through clever messaging, or exploiting psychological vulnerabilities—are no longer as effective in a world where consumers have access to vast amounts of information and are increasingly wary of corporate agendas. In this new paradigm, transparency and authenticity become paramount. Consumers want to engage with brands that they trust, that align with their values, and offer real value beyond the transactional nature of buying and selling. It's no longer just about pushing products but building relationships based on mutual respect and understanding. texts.full_text: the diversity and open-mindedness of a school community are influenced by a multitude of factors beyond just geographical location. While it's true that certain regions may have a more homogenous population, this does not inherently mean that the local schools cannot foster an inclusive environment. Firstly, the administration plays a crucial role in shaping the school culture. They can implement policies and programs that promote diversity and inclusion, such as anti-bullying initiatives, cultural awareness programs, and scholarships for underrepresented groups. Additionally, they can actively recruit and support teachers from diverse backgrounds who can bring different perspectives to the classroom. Furthermore, the student body itself has a significant impact on the school's culture. Students are not passive recipients of their environment; they actively contribute to it through their interactions, attitudes, and actions. texts.full_text: note Established in February1968 at the initiative of President Mobutu, the UEAC linked Zaire to the Central African Republic and Chad, with the broader aim of promoting theestablishment of a Central African Common Market. The Central African Republic withdrew from the Union on January 1, 1993. Despite its name change in 1972 from the Upper Volta andLower Congo to Zaire (and later again toDemocratic Republic of the Congo), UEAC's acronym was not altered. Its headquarters are located in Brazzaville, capital of the Republic of Congo. The UEAC aimedatpromoting economic integration amongits members throughcoordinated policy-makingon trade, transport, energy,agriculture and other sectors that impact regional development significantly. The organization also focused on enhancing inter-state relations, cultural exchanges and regional peace and security. Over time, however, several challengeshave plagued the Union, includingpolitical instabilitywithin member states, lack ofeconomic integration due to disparities in developmental levels among members, as well as differences in governancepractices that often hampered collective decision-making. Asa result, while some areas saw improved cooperation, particularly on peacekeeping efforts and conflictresolution mechanisms, othersremained marked by underdevelopment and limitedinteraction. texts.full_text: remember, I have had a passion for health and fitness. I would watch the fitness channel on television and do the fitness routines in my living room. I always admired the fitness levels and physiques of the fitness instructors, and envisioned myself being as fit as those instructors. I thought about joining a gym for years, but did not think I had the time. Also, I was very intimidated at the thought of walking into the gym for the first time and not knowing where to start. It wasn’t until I was in my thirties that I started working out in a gym. Photo Credit: Amir Marandi Life Gets Serious And So Do I It all started at age 29 when I began having pain shooting up and down both my arms. Having no idea of what was causing the pain, I decided to go into a walk-in clinic. The doctor ordered an MRI of my neck and back and the results showed that I had advanced disk disease with bulging disks in C3, C4, and C7. I was referred immediately to an orthopedic surgeon who told me I needed surgery to fuse my cervical spine. Needless to say, I was shocked! I had not seen this coming, since I was very athletic and active at this point. Not only that, but when he described how complicated the procedure would be and what it involved in order for him to reach those bulging disks by going up one way, detouring around my voice box, down into another canal behind my throat where a blood vessel sits on there just above my heart, which had I lost this vein during surgery that was running from top of neck down under my breast then he would have to take that same vein out through the other site. After all of this happened inside my throat they still had to come into my back and open me up in there as well for this procedure on two different sites within an approximate total of 9-10 hrs in surgery, which is extremely long

texts.full_text: are largely intact in obese mice, the upregulation of IL-23, a cytokine upstream of IL-22, is compromised during the infection. Consequently, these mice are susceptible to C. rodentium infection, and both exogenous IL-22 and IL-23 are able to restore the mucosal host defence. Importantly, we further unveil unexpected functions of IL-22 in regulating metabolism. Mice deficient in IL-22 receptor and fed with high-fat diet are prone to developing metabolic disorders. Strikingly, administration of exogenous IL-22 in genetically obese leptin-receptor-deficient (db/db) mice and mice fed with high-fat diet reverses many of the metabolic symptoms, including hyperglycaemia and insulin resistance. IL-22 shows diverse metabolic benefits, as it improves insulin sensitivity, preserves gut mucosal barrier and endocrine functions, decreases endotoxaemia and chronic inflammation, and regulates lipid metabolism in liver and adipose tissues. In summary, we identify the IL-22 pathway as a novel target for therapeutic intervention in metabolic diseases.

texts.full_text: distribute child pornography merits a fifteen-year mandatory minimum sentence. (Actually distributing child porn, by the way, will get you five). United States v. Williams, 2011 WL 5084645 (9th Cir. Oct. 27, 2011), decision available here. Players: Hard-fought appeal by Reno AFPD Michael Powell. Decision by Judge Thomas, joined by Judge NR Smith and visiting C.D.J. Solomon Oliver, Jr.. Facts: Williams used peer-to-peer software to post and share over 5,000 images of child porn. Id. After FBI agents traced these images to Williams, they searched his home and found a number of suspicious items related to boys. Id. Williams confessed he used internet networks to view and share child porn. Id. He was indicted on possession, distribution, and (important to this appeal) “advertising the distribution of child pornography in violation of 18 U.S.C. § 2251(d)(1)(A).” Id. Williams moved to dismiss the advertising count, “arguing that the statute only applies to individuals who either advertise to produce child pornography or advertise child pornography that they actually produced.” Id. The district court denied the motion; Williams entered a conditional plea to those charges and reserved his right to appeal on this issue. Issue: Whether 18 U.S.C. § 2251(d)(1)(A) applies only to individuals who advertise with intent to produce child pornography or if it also covers advertising the distribution of any existing child pornography. Holding: The Ninth Circuit held that “the statute is not limited in its application but instead reaches anyone ‘who knowingly solicits, entices, transports . . . , provides assistance for another person’ to engage in sexually explicit conduct with a minor and who does so using materials which have been mailed or shipped by any means affecting interstate commerce.” The court further clarified that the statute's language “advertises an offer” encompasses both offers to produce new child pornography as well as existing material.
